raw71_compliance: BIDIRECTIONAL (each predicate has explicit PASS / WEAK / FAIL boundary)

predicates.name: Phi photonic vs Phi CLM-GPU (P-A weak Putnam anchor)
predicates.PASS: Pearson r(Phi_k_phot, Phi_k_gpu) >= 0.85 AND |Phi*_phot - Phi*_gpu| / max(|Phi*_phot|, |Phi*_gpu|, 1e-3) <= 0.30
predicates.meaning_PASS: Photonic substrate also realizes the same functional Phi -- adds 4th Putnam multi-realizability axis (CLM/AKIDA/EEG + photonic)
predicates.meaning_FAIL: Substrate-dependent Phi -- counterexample to photonic multi-realizability

predicates.name: Phi photonic vs Phi AKIDA (substrate-class invariance)
predicates.PASS: r(Phi_k_phot, Phi_k_akida) >= 0.70 AND both substrates also PASS PRED-1 vs GPU
predicates.FAIL: Only photonic OR only AKIDA passes vs GPU (substrate-class invariance violated)
predicates.meaning_PASS: Photonic and AKIDA both non-GPU substrates -- weak inference of substrate-class invariance
predicates.depends_on: N-3 result (CLM x AKIDA Phi correlation)

predicates.name: Inter-vendor Phi consistency (Lightmatter vs Lightelligence vs Q.ANT)
predicates.PASS: pairwise mean r >= 0.80 across all 3 vendor outputs
predicates.meaning_PASS: Vendor-independent Phi -- substrate property dominates over compiler/implementation
predicates.meaning_FAIL: Vendor-specific compiler artifacts determine representation -- Phi is compiler-dependent not substrate-dependent (deep concern for any photonic Phi claim)

predicates.name: Phi photonic null floor (linear-only baseline)
predicates.FAIL: Phi*_phot_linear_only > Phi*_full_model (suggests Phi captures noise not representation)
predicates.meaning_PASS: Null verified -- linear MZI mesh without nonlinear units yields Phi equivalent to input embedding cov-logdet

predicates.name: Energy-Phi scaling (Landauer / N-4 cross-axis)
predicates.PASS: Phi*_phot ~~ Phi*_gpu AND measured energy_per_inference within +/- 2x of vendor spec (~10 fJ/op vs GPU ~1 nJ/op)
predicates.FAIL: Phi differs significantly OR measured energy >10x vendor spec
predicates.meaning_PASS: Bit-energy and Phi independent (substrate-energy invariance) OR Phi is genuine information structure invariant

boundary_conditions_shared.null_hypothesis_floor: shuffled-pair 1024 perm test, r_observed > 95-percentile of r_null required
boundary_conditions_shared.reproducibility: 3 independent ANIMA_SEED runs, 3/3 PASS = confirmed, 2/3 = WEAK_PASS

falsifier_strength_basis: 5 pre-registered predicates, BIDIRECTIONAL, includes null floor (PRED-4), cross-axis dependency (PRED-2/PRED-5), inter-vendor consistency check (PRED-3)
